class.name: AutoworkSpy
class.experimental: Some methods may not be implemented and may be changed in future versions.
class.brief_description: Records and inspects method calls during tests.
class.description:
The AutoworkSpy is used to track and analyze method calls made on objects during testing.
It records each invocation, including the target object, method name, and arguments passed. This allows tests to verify that specific methods were called, how many times they were called, and with what parameters.

method.name: add_call
method.description: Stores the call information for later inspection, including the [param object], [param method_name], and arguments passed in [param args].

method.name: call_count
method.description:
Returns the number of times a method was called.
If [param args] is provided, only calls matching the given arguments are counted. Otherwise, all calls to the specified method are included.

method.name: clear
method.description:
Clears all recorded method calls.
After calling this method, the spy returns to an empty state with no stored invocation history.

method.name: get_call_parameters
method.description:
Returns the parameters used in recorded method calls.
If [param index] is provided, returns the arguments for that specific call instance. If [param index] is -1, returns all recorded argument sets for the method.

method.name: was_called
method.description:
Returns [code]true[/code] if the specified method was called.
If [param args] is provided, the check is refined to match calls with those exact arguments. Otherwise, any call to the method is considered a match.
